compiled_state.title: As a project maintainer, I can run LLM-powered code review on PRs with configurable model tiers
compiled_state.author: Test
compiled_state.description:
## Description

**What**: Replace the CI workflow placeholder review job with a real LLM review using Anthropic claude-code-action, with 3-tier model configuration via GitHub Variables.
**Why**: LLM review is the highest-value check — it catches logic errors, security issues, and design problems that lint and tests miss.
**Scope**:
- IN: claude-code-action integration, API key from Secrets, model IDs from Variables (3 tiers: light, standard, frontier), configurable timeout (default 10 min), variable validation, error handling (401/404/empty)
- OUT: Ruleset configuration (Story 2), onboarding (Story 4), local review gate changes (Epic B)

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, the CI review job uses claude-code-action with API key from GitHub Secrets and model ID from GitHub Variables, passing when no critical findings are found and exiting non-zero otherwise
  ← Satisfies: "PRs require CI status checks to pass including LLM-based code review; the LLM review check passes when the review completes with no findings at severity critical"
- When this story is complete, each of the 3 tiers (light, standard, frontier) has an independently configurable model ID variable, with provider hardcoded to Anthropic
  ← Satisfies: "each of the 3 tiers has an independently configurable model ID variable"
- When this story is complete, the review job validates required variables at startup and surfaces clear diagnostic messages for common failures (401 stale key, 404 invalid model, empty variable)
  ← Satisfies: "LLM review job includes a configurable timeout"
- When this story is complete, unit tests are written and passing for all new or modified logic

## Considerations

- [Reliability] The LLM review job must have an explicit timeout-minutes to prevent indefinite blocking on API hangs.
- [Testing] claude-code-action is a third-party Action — pin by SHA for security. Test seams needed for unit testing without live API calls.

## Escalation Policy

**Escalation policy**: Escalate to the user whenever you do not have high confidence in your understanding of the work, approach, or intent. "High confidence" means clear evidence from the codebase or ticket context — not inference or reasonable assumption. When in doubt, stop and ask rather than guess.
